gazette: General Directorate of Trade Research and Risk Assessment
title: Extended Producer Responsibility has been introduced for textile products in the Netherlands
bodyHtml:
Starting from July 1, 2023, an extended producer responsibility (Uitgebreide Producenten Verantwoordelijkheid, UPV-EPR) has been introduced for textile products in the Netherlands.

According to the announcement from the Commercial Attaché in The Hague, textile products play an important role in Turkey's exports to the Netherlands. Therefore, it is recommended that companies engaged in trade with Dutch firms, including home textiles, workwear, clothing, and textile fiber products, follow the new regulations and fulfill their registration and notification obligations starting from July 1, 2023.

Clothing and home textile manufacturers based outside the Netherlands who directly deliver to end-users (including online providers) are required to appoint a legal entity or individual as their authorized representative in the Netherlands. This authorized representative is responsible for ensuring compliance with the obligations of the "producer."

These obligations only apply to the following newly manufactured textile products:

Clothing:

Consumer clothing

Work clothing, including safety clothing

Home textiles:

Table linen

Bedding

Household linen, such as hand and tea towels

These obligations do not apply to other textile products such as shoes, belts, hats, blankets, bed covers, curtains, and cleaning cloths.

For more information on extended producer responsibility from the Dutch Ministry of the Environment, click here.

To access the decree dated April 14, 2023, containing the rules regarding extended producer responsibility for textile products, click here.
